title: Advanced Python Concepts and Best Practices
sub: Functions · OOP · Decorators · Generators · Exception Handling · Logging

h: OOP — constructor, instance variables, instance methods
Exception handling — try, except, finally, custom exceptions
m: Lambda expressions with filter, map, reduce
Types of variables and methods — instance, class, static

qs.t: Explain different types of arguments in python function with example.
Explain lambda expressions. How do you use lambda expressions in filter, map and reduce. Explain with example.
What are decorators and generators in python. Explain with example.
Explain exception handling in python with try, except and finally. Also show customized exceptions in python.
Explain python logging with different types of logging levels. Also write a program to write exception information to the log file.
Why do you need Assertion in python. Differentiate between exception handling and assertions.
Why do you need __super__ function in python? Explain with example.
Explain different types of variables and methods in python with examples.
Differentiate between IS-A relationship and HAS-A relationship in python with example.
Explain different types of inheritance in python with examples.

Create a class named Student with attributes such as name, age, and grade. Write a program to create multiple objects of the class and display their details using a method. Your program should contain constructor, instance variable and instance methods.

Define a class Dog with attributes name and breed. Include a method bark() that prints a sound. Create objects of the class and demonstrate how the method works. Your program should contain constructor, instance variable and instance methods.

Create a class Rectangle that has attributes length and width. Implement methods to calculate the area and perimeter of the rectangle and display the results. Your program should contain constructor, instance variable and instance methods.

Create a class with different number of attributes and behavior. Your class must contain constructor. Create two objects and initialize with different values. Call all the methods you create. Define instance method and instance variable with example.
Explain OOP in Python. Create a class DataProcessor with methods for loading, cleaning, and saving data.
Write short notes on: OOP · Iterators Vs Generators · Modules and packages · *args and **kwargs

title: Data Sources and APIs
sub: Data types · Databases · REST · SOAP · Web Scraping · Chunking

h: REST API — HTTP methods, REST vs SOAP
BeautifulSoup web scraping — steps and practical extraction
Databases — relational vs non-relational, access methods, ORM
m: Reading and writing PDF and Excel files in Python
l: Types of data — structured, unstructured, semi-structured

qs.t: Explain different types of datas.
How do you read and write pdf and excel file? Explain with python example.
Explain types of databases (relational and non relational) in detail with their characteristics. How do you access these databases? Also compare with python ORM.
Define API. Also explain its communication flow.
Differentiate between REST Vs SOAP API.
Describe REST APIs and their HTTP methods.
Explain the steps to scrap data from public web page using Beautiful Soup.

The website 'suryapatro.com' contains 100 astrologer records with the attributes id, name, address, and affiliation. The attributes id, name, and address contain single values, while affiliation contains multiple values. Extract 30 records ranging from id 50 to 80 using the Requests and BeautifulSoup libraries.
Write Short note on: (A) Chunking (B) Lazy Evaluation

title: Advanced Data Wrangling and Transformation
sub: Pandas · Missing Values · Feature Engineering · Data Cleaning Pipeline

h: Data cleaning pipeline — components and reusable class design
Handling special data types — missing values, categorical, time-series
m: Merging, Joining, Reshaping and Pivoting in Pandas

qs.t: Explain Merging, Joining, Reshaping and Pivoting in pandas with example.
How do you handle missing values in python?
How do you handle categorical, timeseries data in python? Explain with example.
Explain feature transformation, encoding, and scaling operations.
Explain the various components involved in a data cleaning pipeline.
Design a reusable data-cleaning pipeline class (Assume necessary data if needed).
Write short note on: Memory Optimization.

title: Applied Statistics and Exploratory Analysis
sub: Covariance · Skewness · Correlation · Kurtosis · EDA · Auto-EDA

h: EDA — workflow automation with full code
Statistical measures — covariance, skewness, correlation, kurtosis

qs.t: Explain covariance, skewness, correlation and kurtosis with example. (Use necessary python package)
What is exploratory data analysis (EDA)? How do you automate EDA work flow? Explain in detail with codes. Your workflow should contain all necessary function.
What is Auto-EDA? Compare ydata-profiling and sweetviz libraries.
Write short note on: (a) Linear Regression (b) Trend Analysis (c) Descriptive Vs Inferential Statistics (d) Hypothesis testing

title: Data Visualization and Storytelling
sub: Matplotlib · Seaborn · Plotly · Dashboard · Colors · Storytelling

h: Plotly — interactive dashboard, static vs interactive, when to use Plotly
Dashboard design — KPIs, trends, comparisons, dashboard thinking
m: Matplotlib vs Seaborn vs Plotly — comparison with box plot and pair plot

qs.t: Explain principles of effective visualization.
Compare Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Plotly. Create box plot and pair plot using Seaborn.
Write code for an interactive Plotly dashboard with trends, pie chart, and bar chart.
Compare static vs interactive visualizations. When to use Plotly over Matplotlib?
Explain dashboard thinking. Design a notebook dashboard with KPIs, trends, and comparisons.

Explain the importance of colors in visualization. A city authority collects hourly traffic volume, accident count, accident severity, location (junction-wise). Which visualization approach best reveals high-risk zones and peak hours? Explain with pros and cons of visualization approach you suggest.
Write Short notes on: (a) Create subplots with line, bar, and histogram (b) Key principles of data storytelling (c) Visualization driven insight generation.

title: Data Engineering and Automation
sub: ETL Pipeline · DAGs · Logging · Schedulers · Report Generation

h: ETL pipeline — design, implement, logging, monitoring, error handling
Automated report generation — HTML, Excel, PDF
m: Data storage and retrieval strategies in pipelines

qs.t: Design and implement ETL pipeline of your own.
Describe logging, monitoring and error handling in ETL pipelines.
Explain about different data storages and retrieval strategies in data pipelines.
What are DAGs in data pipelines? Explain task dependencies.
Describe automated report generation. Write a function that generates HTML report.

A growing online education platform wants to track student activity, test performance, and revenue. Generate daily and monthly analytics reports automatically. Deliver reports in Excel (operations), HTML (dashboard), and PDF (management) formats. Eliminate manual reporting work.
Write short notes on: (a) Data Science Vs Data Engineering (b) ETL Vs ELT (c) Scheduler Vs CRON
